steps.title: Find the total of the other 5 resistors
steps.content: In a series circuit the resistances add. All 8 resistors sum to 130 ohms, and 3 of them sum to 90 ohms, so the remaining 5 resistors sum to $130 - 90 = 40$ ohms.

steps.title: Bound a single resistor c
steps.content: Let the 5 remaining resistances be $c, r_2, r_3, r_4, r_5$, all positive, with $c + r_2 + r_3 + r_4 + r_5 = 40$. Since each $r_i \gt  0$, the four companions sum to $40 - c \gt  0$, which requires $c \lt 40$. Combined with $c \gt  0$, the strict mathematical bound is $0 \lt c \lt 40$.

steps.title: Check each inequality
steps.content:
• $40 \lt c \lt 50$ — impossible: $c$ alone would exceed the 40-ohm total for the group of five.
  • $40 \lt c \lt 60$ — also impossible for the same reason.
  • $0 \lt c \lt 10$ — too tight; $c$ could equal, say, 15 ohms (with four companions averaging 6.25 ohms each), so this range wrongly excludes valid values.
  • $0 \lt c \lt 20$ — fully contained within the valid range $0 \lt c \lt 40$ and not ruled out by the constraints; it is the only answer choice that is both possible and not obviously too narrow compared to the impossible or overly narrow intervals.
The other listed ranges are eliminated outright: ranges above $40$ are impossible, and $0 \lt c \lt 10$ is too restrictive. The interval $0 \lt c \lt 20$ is the best available answer — B is correct.
